title: The Art of Star Wars Episode IV: A New Hope
content:
{{Top|rwm|leg|ref|italics=1}} {{ReferenceBook |image=[[File:Art_of_Star_wars.jpg]] |title=''The Art of Star Wars Episode IV:
A New Hope'' |author= |cover artist=[[Ralph McQuarrie]] |illustrator=Ralph McQuarrie, various |editor=[[Carol Titelman]] |publisher=[[Del Rey]] |release date=*[[November]] [[1979]] {{C|Original}}''[[Star Wars Year By Year: A Visual History, Updated and Expanded Edition]]'' *[[October]] [[1994]] {{C|Reissued}} *[[January]] [[1997]] {{C|Special Edition}} |type=Softcover |pages=192 |dimensions=12.0 × 9.1 × 0.5 inches |weight=1.8 pounds |isbn=0-345-39202-7 |series= |preceded by= |followed by= }} '''''The Art of Star Wars Episode IV: A New Hope''''' includes hundreds of sketches, storyboards, matte paintings, blueprints, production paintings, and costume designs that illustrate the original screenplay. Originally published as '''''The Art of Star Wars''''' in [[November]] [[1979]], the book was reissued in [[October]] [[1994]] with the expanded title, with an update on [[January 14]], [[1997]] to include material from the [[The Star Wars Trilogy Special Edition|Special Edition]]. It includes a majority of artwork from [[Ralph McQuarrie]], from the initial storyboards to the international theatrical posters. It includes early sketches and designs for the main characters of the film and offers an insight into what [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] might have looked like. The book also includes a version of the movie script. ==Publisher's summary== ''A New Hope ''was part of the original title of the movie that became'' Star Wars'', the ultimate movie entertainment experience of the 1970's that lives on as one of the most-loved movies of all time. ''The Art Of Star Wars ''contains the complete script by George Lucas of the first movie, beautifully illustrated with the movie's fantastic works of art. In this unique compilation of all the imagination and beauty that went into the beginning of the film trilogy, the magic of'' Star Wars ''lives on.'''' ==Contents== *Section 1: The Script *Section 2: Poster Art *Section 3: Spin-Off Cartoons and Fan Art ==Media== ===Cover gallery=== File:TheArtofStarWars1977.jpg|Original edition cover File:Art_of_Star_Wars_Pl.jpg|Polish cover – ''Gwiezdne Wojny Epizod IV: Nowa Nadzieja Album'' File:Ja_Art_of_Star_wars.png|Japanese cover – アート・オブ・スター・ウォーズ 新たなる希望≪特別篇≫ ==Sources== {{Mediacat|from=1|imagecat=Images from The Art of Star Wars Episode IV: A New Hope}} *''[[The Jedi Master's Quizbook]]'' *''[[The Secrets of Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire]]'' *{{Lucasfilm|url=news/history-in-objects-the-art-of-star-wars|text=History in Objects: ''The Art of Star Wars''}} *{{InsiderCite|219|The Art of Star Wars (article)|The Art of ''Star Wars''|reprint=1}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{ArtOf}} {{Interlang |de=The Art of Star Wars: Episode IV – Eine neue Hoffnung#legends |ja=アート・オブ・スター・ウォーズ 新たなる希望≪特別篇≫ |ka=ვარსკვლავური ომები, ეპიზოდი IV: ახალი იმედი: მხატვრობა |pl=Nowa nadzieja (album) |sr=The Art of Star Wars Episode IV: A New Hope }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Art of Star Wars Episode IV: A New Hope}} [[Category:Illustrated Screenplays]] [[Category:Legends art books|Episode IV]]
